message:
feat(clusters): surface remove-cluster button in cluster detail modal (#5901) (#6097)

Follow-up to #5908: the trash-icon button in the cluster card header was
easy to miss. Add a discoverable 'Remove cluster' action in two places on
the cluster detail modal:

- A compact trash icon next to the Rename button in the modal header
- A prominent red 'Cluster is offline' banner with a 'Remove cluster from
  kubeconfig' CTA just below the cluster status details

Both affordances are gated on the same conditions as the card button:
the cluster must be unreachable AND kubeconfig-backed AND the local agent
must be connected (so the /kubeconfig/remove endpoint can be reached).
Clicking either closes the detail modal and opens the existing
RemoveClusterDialog confirmation flow, which calls the backend endpoint
(RemoveContext / POST /kubeconfig/remove, added in #5658).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3016,6 +3016,8 @@
     "noIssuesToRepair": "No issues to repair",
     "askTitle": "Ask AI any question about this cluster",
     "renameCluster": "Rename cluster",
+    "offlineRemoveTitle": "Cluster is offline",
+    "offlineRemoveDesc": "This cluster cannot be reached. If the underlying cluster no longer exists, remove its kubeconfig entry to clean up your cluster list. This does not affect the cluster itself.",
     "openConsole": "Open {{provider}} console",
     "akaLabel": "aka:",
     "alsoKnownAs": "Also known as: {{aliases}}",
